description: Hypothesis: replace NUR input with nixpkgs crush package and remove NUR overlay from flake overlays.

asi.hypothesis: NUR adds flake indirection and lock overhead; switching to nixpkgs.crush should lower benchmark.
asi.rollback_reason: Iteration was slower than baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Try platform-specific partitioning of integration tests before reverting this change.

description: Iteration 3 hypothesis: partition integration-configurations-eval assertions by platform to avoid evaluating non-native hosts in each check.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping non-native host assertions in integration test should reduce evaluation work on each platform.
asi.rollback_reason: First-run wall time increased vs baseline (8.8s vs 5.8s) with subsequent 0.2s hot-cache run; not a stable improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: If wanting to continue, gather colder metrics via fresh worker or compare from clean cache before deciding; otherwise keep check split but maybe not this way.

description: Iteration 4 hypothesis: avoid eager eval of multi-system checks by switching to explicit checks attrset with dynamic keys instead of genAttrs.

asi.hypothesis: Using explicit checks attrset should evaluate only aarch64-darwin checks when that path is selected, reducing evaluation work.
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement in first sampled run (6.7s vs 5.8s baseline); subsequent warmed run not indicative.
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing expensive evaluation in integration test by avoiding repeated self reference patterns, e.g., passing `self` not as full attrset.

description: Hypothesis: split integration-configurations-eval into platform-selected assertions via evalScope, passing only platform configs from tests/default so each check evaluates only relevant branch.

asi.hypothesis: Conditionally selecting darwin/linux assertion sets should avoid evaluating non-native host configs in the integration test on each check platform.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep darwin/linux split and consider passing only needed attrs to other heavy tests as well (or caching shared helpers) for incremental gains.

description: Hypothesis: pass explicit integration eval inputs only via *_Input and *_SystemInput and avoid resolving unused cross-platform configs by gating config bindings on selectedScope.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding unconditional cross-platform self lookups and unused fallback bindings should reduce evaluation work on Darwin check.
asi.next_action_hint: Also avoid passing duplicate *_SystemInput if unused by refactoring to use config objects directly or a mode-specific attrset.
asi.rollback_hint: If regression appears on Linux path, keep previous branching with platform-specific args but retain explicit input names.

description: Hypothesis: pass full platform configuration objects into integration test and derive both config/system from that object, eliminating duplicate *_Config/*_System input plumbing.

asi.hypothesis: Passing whole nixos/darwin configuration attrsets (instead of separate Config vs System args) reduces attrset argument churn and avoids duplicated evaluation paths, while keeping scope gating explicit.
asi.next_action_hint: Run the same scoped benchmark on Linux CI matrix when available, then consider folding home-check generation into a narrower machine-specific function to further trim eval graph for each scope.

description: Hypothesis: split nixos host assertions by native architecture (x86_64-linux vs aarch64-linux) while passing only relevant host configs in non-Darwin test invocation.

asi.hypothesis: Evaluating all nixos hosts together still forces more checks on each Linux system; scope by host family should reduce per-system eval work and avoid unnecessary cross-host attr forcing.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed slower runtime on Darwin validation run (5.4s vs best 4.3s); also changes Linux coverage shape and test names.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert split or keep a simpler scoped list while preserving single-host inputs, then explore a different low-level optimization (e.g., avoid reconstructing duplicated package-absence assertions via helper predicates) without changing host partitioning.

description: Hypothesis: pass only platform-relevant Linux host configuration objects from tests/default and make nixosChecks host assertions conditional on non-null host configs to avoid evaluating irrelevant host checks.

asi.hypothesis: If Linux systems only need one host per worker architecture, slicing integration inputs by platform plus conditional check blocks should reduce per-platform evaluation burden and keep Darwin behavior unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: On aarch64-darwin, this change regressed to 7.9s (after cold eval) versus prior best 4.3s, likely due extra list-shape churn from conditional assembly.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer the prior simpler `nixos`-all-hosts path and focus on smaller code-shape optimizations (e.g., avoid `lib.filter`/`builtins.all` helper overhead) if any further improvement is needed.

description: Hypothesis: pass only architecture-relevant Linux host configuration inputs from tests/default (nixos-x86_64-linux vs nixos-aarch64-linux) and make nixosChecks host assertions conditionally include only non-null configs.

asi.hypothesis: Avoid passing unused host configs/hosting cross-platform assertions in each architecture to trim integration eval while keeping Darwin checks unchanged; simpler conditional blocks may reduce both evaluation graph and CI runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: On aarch64-darwin, benchmark regressed to 5.2s vs prior best 4.3s, likely from additional conditional list-shape overhead.
asi.next_action_hint: If this direction is still pursued, benchmark directly on native Linux builders rather than inferring from Darwin; if unavailable, revert and focus on smaller reductions in Darwn path only.

description: Hypothesis: partition nixos checks by architecture via evalScope (`nixos-x86_64-linux` vs `nixos-aarch64-linux`) while retaining full checks inside dedicated nixosChecks variants.

asi.hypothesis: Architectural partitioning of host coverage should reduce eval graph per Linux builder while preserving full Darwin behavior, by using separate `nixosChecksX86`/`nixosChecksAarch64` and selecting by evalScope.
asi.rollback_reason: On this aarch64-darwin benchmark run, added host-partitioning and extra branch structure regressed to 4.7s (from best 4.3s), suggesting list-branch refactor overhead outweighs any savings in this evaluation path.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Darwin-path-centric baseline optimization and defer host-architecture partitioning until native Linux CI measurements are possible.

description: Hypothesis: simplify scope gating in integration test by selecting darwin/nixos path based on whether host-specific inputs are provided, removing evalScope/selectedScope branches and unnecessary host-guarded config extraction.

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating evalScope string dispatch and selectedScope conditionals should reduce eval graph size on both darwin and linux callers by relying on explicit input presence for branch selection.
asi.rollback_reason: Darwin benchmark regressed to 4.6s (vs prior best 4.3s).
asi.next_action_hint: Given limited delta, focus on smaller, orthogonal reductions in nixos host config shape only when native Linux benchmarks are available.

description: Hypothesis: cache home package membership lookups inside homeChecks by precomputing per-host package-name set, avoiding repeated `lib.any` scans for each package assertion.

asi.hypothesis: Repeated `hasPackage` calls in `homeChecks` re-scan `homeConfig.home.packages` for each assertion; building one set of package names per host should reduce eval overhead in configuration checks.
asi.next_action_hint: If this keeps, validate with additional runs (especially on native Linux check variants) to ensure no regression and that package lookups still match expected behavior with unusual package lists.
asi.next_step: Explore similar precompute patterns for `darwinHome`/nixos checks (e.g., program/source maps) and keep only if noise-adjusted gains hold.

description: Hypothesis: cache package membership sets for all relevant host package lists once at file scope (darwin system/home + nixos host home packages) and thread them into homeChecks and nixos assertions, reducing repeated `hasPackage` scans.

asi.hypothesis: The previous optimization moved `hasPackage` to precomputed host-local set checks in homeChecks; extending this to all integration assertions should avoid repeated linear scans and also reduce attrset churn in other package assertions.
asi.rollback_reason: Full integration eval regressed to 4.9s on aarch64-darwin versus 4.3s best, likely due extra package-set construction in branch-unselected paths and/or no material reduction in evaluated checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a narrower variant: keep homeChecks precompute and only replace package assertions in the currently executed check path while avoiding global construction of potentially unused package sets.

description: Hypothesis: replicate the keep optimization on a second run to validate stability of the package-set precompute in `homeChecks`; confirm whether prior 4.3s win is consistent or cache-noise.

asi.hypothesis: Run #13 achieved 4.3s, but without external warm/cold control and on this host repeated runs may vary; this run tests repeatability before proceeding to additional structural changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Second replicate regressed to 4.9s, suggesting the 4.3s result may have been a cold-cache or transient fluctuation.
asi.next_action_hint: Do one additional warm run with explicit cache clear strategy if possible, then consider whether to keep the optimization or return to previous baseline for safe improvements; avoid making further changes on potentially noisy data.

description: Hypothesis: avoid the two-pass home package set build in `homeChecks` by deriving the name set in one pass (`map` from package derivations directly).

asi.hypothesis: Current keep path still rebuilds home package names via `map lib.getName` then another `map` to attrs; removing one intermediate list may shave tiny eval overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Single-pass refactor regressed to 5.0s versus best-known, indicating effect is within/noise and likely not beneficial.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to the stable known-good state and validate cross-platform behavior before further micro-optimizations.

description: Hypothesis: cache darwin system package list as a set in the darwinChecks branch (and reuse it for dropbox/hammerspoon assertions) while keeping `hasPackage` polymorphic to support existing list-based call sites.

asi.hypothesis: Selective package-set precompute in the hot Darwin check path should avoid repeated linear scans for system-package assertions with minimal extra overhead, while keeping homeChecks’ local package set optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed regression to 5.3s on this benchmark run; additional set conversion cost outweighed any gain versus prior baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Given several regressions after this line of package-set refactors, consider freezing package-membership optimization scope and exploring other independent micro-optimizations (e.g., helper/lists in assertion assembly) only if they are expected to reduce structural overhead.

description: Hypothesis: cache darwin system packages as a single attrset within `darwinChecks` and use set lookups for `dropbox`/`hammerspoon` assertions, keeping the keeped `homeChecks` package precompute untouched.

asi.hypothesis: This targets the two repeated system-package linear scans in the active Darwin branch with one-time set construction; if effective, it should reduce a small amount of repeated traversal in the hot path.
asi.rollback_reason: On this run, latency regressed to 4.5s vs best-known 4.3s from the prior package-cache keep, so regression is still above the best baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to the minimal keep state (`homeChecks` package set only) and explore a non-overlapping micro-optimization (e.g., local `homeProgram` aliases) if additional gains are still sought.

description: Hypothesis: re-run the kept `homeChecks` package-set optimization from run #13 as a clean repetition to validate stability before further changes.

asi.hypothesis: The prior 4.3s result may have been influenced by cache state; this run checks whether the keep gains hold on a fresh sequence.
asi.rollback_reason: Did not reproduce 4.3s target; runtime regressed to 4.4s, so the prior keep should not be considered stable enough to adopt alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Either continue with a different structural optimization (e.g., minimize repeated attribute traversals inside `homeChecks`) or defer until Linux-native measurements are available.

description: Hypothesis: hoist repeated Home Manager sub-attribute accesses inside `homeChecks` into local bindings (`homeData`, `homePrograms`, `homeActivation`, `homeXdg`) to reduce repeated attr-path lookup overhead.

asi.hypothesis: `homeChecks` is invoked for every checked host and currently performs many repeated `homeConfig.programs`, `homeConfig.home`, and `homeConfig.home.activation` lookups; local bindings could shave small amounts of eval work in this hot path.
asi.next_action_hint: If kept, validate on another run and then on a Linux-native check target to ensure improvements are stable and not Darwin-only noise.

description: Hypothesis: hoist repeated NixOS host-level attribute paths in `nixosChecks` into local bindings (`wslPrograms`, `wslNetworking`, etc.) to reduce repeated attr-path lookups in the hot Linux assertions.

asi.hypothesis: `nixosChecks` reads `wslConfig.programs`, `networking`, and `wsl`/`x230`/`vm` fields repeatedly; introducing local aliases should shorten Nix expression traversal and lower eval cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Did not improve—runtime regressed substantially to 6.1s, likely due larger attrset/lambda let expansion and extra host binding noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to #20 keep state and resume micro-opts only in areas with lower risk of expanding bound variable graph; additionally, run a second validation pass of keep without further edits to confirm true floor.

description: Hypothesis: replicate the current keep change from #20 (home sub-attribute aliasing) to validate stability on a clean run after reverting a failed variant.

asi.hypothesis: If the 4.3s result from #20 is stable, it should hold on repeated runs; this run checks for regression/noise after intervening failed edits.
asi.rollback_reason: Second replicate returned 4.7s, indicating significant variance and making the 4.3s outcome less stable than initially observed.
asi.next_action_hint: Given unstable timing without code changes, keep #20 only if we can justify as best of available samples or revert to earlier stable keep if a more reproducible candidate appears; prioritize testing variants with larger structural differences or skip further micro-opts due noise.

description: Hypothesis: in `nixosChecks`, precompute package-name attrsets for `wslHome`, `x230Home`, and `vmHome` only for Linux package assertions, replacing three repeated linear `hasPackage` scans with `builtins.hasAttr` lookups.

asi.hypothesis: Package-assertion hotspots in nixosChecks (`sourcegraph-amp`, `zed-nightly-bin`) were rewritten to O(1) attr lookups after building tiny local sets, which should reduce list scans for each host.
asi.rollback_reason: Did not improve and regressed to 5.3s; set-construction overhead outweighed any list-scan reduction in this evaluation path.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to keep state and avoid further micro-optimizations in hot loops unless larger structural improvements are identified; prioritize benchmarking with a stable methodology (e.g., controlled cache state) if available.

description: Hypothesis: add further local binding for `homePrograms.agent-skills` (`homeSkillConfig`) inside `homeChecks` to avoid repeated deep agent-skills attribute-path traversals across many assertions.

asi.hypothesis: `homeChecks` contains many repeated accesses of `homePrograms.agent-skills`; binding this subtree once should marginally cut repeated attr lookups in the hot per-host home-check path.
asi.rollback_reason: Result was slower (4.6s) than prior best on this state, so this deeper aliasing did not help in current environment.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop accumulating nested local aliases unless larger gains observed; focus on meaningful structural changes or stabilize measurement methodology.

description: Hypothesis: run a third clean replication of #20 keep state to check for transient 4.3s result and confirm variance before pursuing further modifications.

asi.hypothesis: Previous two repeats of the current keep showed 4.4–4.7s; another run may indicate if 4.3s was a one-off cache artifact before deciding to continue optimization or hold.
asi.rollback_reason: Third replicate remains 4.7s; 4.3s remains a likely noise artifact rather than stable improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Given repeated instability and no clear beneficial changes, pivot to a more substantial optimization candidate (if any) or pause to avoid overfitting to benchmark noise.

description: Hypothesis: run the same scoped benchmark on a native Linux output (`checks.x86_64-linux.integration-configurations-eval`) to validate whether prior keep/state changes hold on Linux and avoid Darwin-only noise.

asi.hypothesis: Native Linux timing should better reflect NixOS-side gains from the integration-configurations-eval refactors and provide a lower-noise signal for host-scope optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: Build failed due platform mismatch in `home-manager.users.martinfan.programs.agent-skills.bundlePath` while evaluating x86_64-linux check on aarch64-darwin (`agent-skills-source` drv requires x86_64-linux).
asi.next_action_hint: Re-run this on a native Linux CI runner instead of emulated/macOS cross-system checkout; defer Linux-only optimization validation until that environment is available.

description: Hypothesis: force-disable the Nix eval cache during the integration check run to remove build-time cache effects and check whether the prior 4.x results were cache artifacts.

asi.hypothesis: Repeated unstable times suggest cache warmness is influencing variance; disabling eval-cache should produce a cleaner signal for whether `homeChecks` precompute changes truly improve evaluation speed.
asi.rollback_reason: This run used `--option eval-cache false`, which changes measurement conditions relative to normal CI and should not be treated as a production metric by itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Follow with a comparable default-cache run after a cold/clean invocation (or after restarting daemon) to decide if any keep candidates are truly stable.

description: Hypothesis: run a matched default-cache control immediately after a previous `--option eval-cache false` run to test if the `4.3s–4.5s` outliers were cache artifacts and recover a representative baseline.

asi.hypothesis: The prior no-cache run changed measurement conditions; a follow-up default-cache run in the same session checks whether the apparent speed gains are robust under normal CI-equivalent settings.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.integration-configurations-eval --no-link` as the comparison baseline, then only pursue further code edits if default runs are consistently below 5.8 for multiple iterations.

description: Hypothesis: perform a direct replicate of the default-cache control run (run #28 equivalent) to estimate noise and confirm the post-no-cache stabilization signal.

asi.hypothesis: If the control run is stable under repeated execution, `integration-configurations-eval` has a real lower floor than 5.8; replication is needed to distinguish cache/cache-state artifacts from structural gains.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more replicate and only pursue code changes if at least two consecutive default-cache runs stay below 5.0 on this host; otherwise treat 5.0–5.8 as noise envelope and stop micro-optimizations.

description: Hypothesis: run one more replicate of the default-cache benchmark to verify whether repeated evaluations have converged near a lower, stable latency under normal settings.

asi.hypothesis: A third replicate reduces uncertainty after the recent 5.6->4.8->4.8 sequence; sustained sub-5s results would justify treating 5.8 as stale baseline for this host and moving on to structural hypotheses.
asi.next_action_hint: If a fourth replicate remains sub-5s, resume structural optimization candidates (e.g., `darwinChecks` path aliasing and reduced package-lookups) on the same branch; otherwise treat this as cache churn noise.

description: Hypothesis: a fourth replicate to confirm convergence; if stable, shift from noise investigation to next structural optimization candidate.

asi.hypothesis: Two consecutive 4.2s runs would imply cache warmth is not the main factor and that the effective baseline on this host is much lower than the historical 5.8s marker.
asi.next_action_hint: Begin testing the most promising structural candidate from `latest next:` history: reintroduce targeted `darwinChecks` local aliasing around repeated attr-path reads, but only if this run also confirms stability.

description: Hypothesis: add targeted `let` aliases in `darwinChecks` for repeated `darwinConfig` sub-atoms (system/programs/networking/services/martin, post-activation text, systemPackages) to reduce repeated deep attr-path lookups in the hot Darwin path.

asi.hypothesis: `darwinChecks` had several repeated `darwinConfig.<path>` reads; hoisting these should cut attr-path traversal while preserving test coverage.
asi.rollback_reason: Did not outperform the current observed 4.2s envelope on this branch and appears to be neutral/noise relative to prior stable replicates; keep a cleaner state without this refactor until a stronger candidate emerges.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer returning to the pre-change state used for 4.2 runs and explore candidates outside hot-path attr rewrites, or validate on a truly cold worker if this still seems potentially beneficial.

description: Hypothesis: re-run the reverted baseline after a discard-edited iteration to confirm the stable 4.2 envelope is not a one-off.

asi.hypothesis: If baseline continues to stay near 4.2 after config resets, this gives confidence that prior improvements are not due the discarded local aliasing.
asi.rollback_reason: This replicate returned 4.5s, indicating significant noise and no clear signal from the exact no-change baseline; no benchmark-guided optimization change yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Collect another zero-edit replicate and then decide whether to attempt a non-`darwinChecks` structural simplification or pause optimization.

description: Hypothesis: perform an additional zero-edit replicate to characterize remaining variance before attempting further structural changes.

asi.hypothesis: Multiple back-to-back runs are needed to separate noise from real gains; if values stay around 4.2–4.7, optimization opportunities are likely overshadowed by environment variance.
asi.rollback_reason: This run was slower (4.7s), consistent with noise and not a stable incremental gain over recent baselines.
asi.next_action_hint: Shift focus to a different measurable structural optimization candidate outside repeated attr-path aliases, or pause and avoid further micro-changes until a clearer signal appears.

description: Hypothesis: reduce intermediate list construction in `homeChecks` by building `homePackageSet` in one pass directly from `homeData.packages` (`map (pkg: ...)` instead of name list + map).

asi.hypothesis: Removing `packageNames = map lib.getName ...` should lower allocation in `homeChecks`, which is executed per-host and contributes to repeated eval cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Replicate this change immediately (2–3 runs) on default-cache settings to confirm reproducibility, since prior data showed sensitivity to cache state.

description: Hypothesis replication: run the one-pass `homePackageSet` implementation again to verify variance and check if the 4.3s result is repeatable.

asi.hypothesis: If this optimization is real, consecutive default-cache runs should stay near 4.2–4.4 on this host without additional code changes.
asi.rollback_reason: N/A
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more replicate; if stable, treat this as the new structural candidate for this branch and consider further small package-membership micro-optimizations only if needed.

description: Hypothesis: third replicate to check stability of the one-pass `homePackageSet` optimization under default cache.

asi.hypothesis: Even with minor variance, if this change remains below historical baseline and close to prior repeats, it likely remains worthwhile.
asi.next_action_hint: With 4.2, 4.2, and 4.5 observed, move on to another low-risk optimization candidate on top of this baseline rather than further replicates.

description: Hypothesis: add a single `homeSkillCfg = homePrograms.agent-skills` binding in `homeChecks` and replace repeated agent-skills path lookups with this local reference for all related assertions.

asi.hypothesis: The previous iteration showed many repeated deep `agent-skills` attr traversals; centralizing this subtree might reduce repeated path evaluation while keeping assertions unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Run regressed to 4.9s, indicating additional local binding and replacements did not help and likely added overhead/noise under current evaluation conditions.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this change and keep focus on other structural candidates; if needed, constrain agent-skills aliasing to a subset of checks only.

description: Hypothesis: add a lightweight Darwin home-package membership cache (`darwinHomePackageSet` + `hasDarwinHomePackage`) and reuse `darwinSystemPackages` aliases for system-package assertions in `darwinChecks`.

asi.hypothesis: `darwin`-path package assertions currently scan home package lists three times and touch activation system packages repeatedly; localizing these lookups should reduce repeated linear scans and attr lookups in this hot branch.
asi.rollback_reason: Introduced overhead from additional let structure and set build; runtime regressed to 4.9s, so this change is not beneficial in practice.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert and continue from one-pass `homePackageSet` baseline. If further package-optimization is needed, prefer low-risk changes that avoid extra per-host set construction in `darwinChecks`.

description: Hypothesis: zero-change validation after revert to confirmed baseline (`homeChecks` one-pass conversion) to re-anchor timing and confirm whether prior 4.2 runs were warm-cache outliers.

asi.hypothesis: This run tests whether the one-pass `homePackageSet` wins are stable without further edits or control over cache state.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed 4.8s, indicating significant variance and weakening confidence in sustained sub-4.3 behavior under this session's cache state.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more baseline replicate; if still above 4.7, proceed with no further structural edits and treat 4.8+ as operating range while waiting for native Linux measurements.

description: Hypothesis: second zero-change baseline replicate to assess whether one-pass set conversion remains a sustained gain in this cache state.

asi.hypothesis: Consistent 4.8–4.9 would indicate the earlier 4.2 numbers were likely transient cache/cache-layout effects rather than repeatable optimization deltas.
asi.rollback_reason: Second replicate landed at 4.9s, so this run did not reinforce the earlier faster baselines; no structural change to keep on this data alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause micro-optimization attempts until the environment is stable (e.g., native Linux runner or fresh worker) and log this as a deferred investigation path.

description: Hypothesis: confirm that native-Linux integration check measurement is still blocked on this host even after subsequent expression edits; validate that failure is environmental/platform-specific, not due current working-tree changes.

asi.hypothesis: Cross-system check invocation on aarch64-darwin should continue to fail with platform-mismatch in the agent-skills bundle path; confirming prevents wasting cycles on Linux-only optimization validation here.
asi.rollback_reason: Run failed due platform mismatch while attempting to evaluate `checks.x86_64-linux.integration-configurations-eval` on an aarch64-darwin machine; no code change to evaluate.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue Linux-specific optimization validation only on native Linux runner; keep aarch64-darwin loop focused on default-cache Darwin checks.

description: Hypothesis: use `nix build --system x86_64-linux` to get Linux baseline timing directly from this host despite prior native-eval mismatch, allowing Linux-side optimization validation without native Linux runner.

asi.hypothesis: Forcing the build system to `x86_64-linux` should bypass earlier platform-mismatch and return a Linux-equivalent benchmark path to validate integration-check deltas.
asi.rollback_reason: Cross-system attempt failed due builder-side error while evaluating `agent-skills-source` derivation (`Undefined error: 0` from bash), so no valid timing comparison for optimization state was obtained.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Darwin baseline loop here until a native Linux environment is available, and avoid consuming budget on non-deterministic cross-system probes unless the Linux runner path is validated.

description: Hypothesis: collect another default-cache Darwin baseline replicate after cross-system probing to confirm we are still in the 4.8–4.9 operational envelope before deciding on further edits.

asi.hypothesis: After failed Linux-probe attempts, a fresh default Darwin replicate should indicate whether recent changes still hold; if stable at ~4.8, environment variance likely dominates tiny sub-5 improvements.
asi.rollback_reason: This run stayed within the recent 4.8-4.9 envelope and does not establish a clear new structural signal.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more replicate only if needed; otherwise defer further edits until a clear, repeatable sub-4.3 gain appears or native Linux context is available.

description: Hypothesis: perform one final short default-cache Darwin replicate before pausing to confirm steady-state timing after repeated control runs and cross-system failures.

asi.hypothesis: A final replicate should show whether the 4.8–4.9 timing is stable; stability would validate no immediate structural path forward on this host.
asi.rollback_reason: Result remained 4.8s; no better than recent baseline and still too noisy to justify further micro-optimization here.
asi.next_action_hint: Hold edits, keep this working branch unchanged, and schedule Linux-native benchmark when a compatible runner is available before attempting additional code-level changes.

description: Hypothesis: another default-cache Darwin replicate after a sequence of 4.8 runs to test cache-thaw behavior; if this hits 4.2, variance may be largely runtime-state dependent.

asi.hypothesis: If this 4.2 result is reproducible, the check appears capable of a much lower floor than recent 4.8 values once caches/JIT state align; no code changes made.
asi.rollback_reason: Result did not establish a consistent trend by itself and is likely another cache-state sample; cannot conclude optimization gain without sustained repeats.
asi.next_action_hint: Run at least one additional replicate immediately; if second run is still ~4.2 then treat current branch as stable and defer edits until structural ideas from Linux-side are available.

description: Hypothesis: a third consecutive default-cache replicate to confirm the 4.2 sample was not a fluke and capture the practical best-case under current evaluation state.

asi.hypothesis: If this and the prior 4.2 run are repeated, the effective benchmark floor on this host may be ~4.1s, making further micro-optimizations potentially low-value without Linux validation.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more replicate; if 4.1 persists, lock this as baseline plateau for this environment and prioritize Linux-native validation before additional edits.
